candidates.evidence_quote: the COVID-19 pandemic continued to affect our business during periods of accelerated case counts
we experienced increased restaurant staff absenteeism
temporary shifts in consumer behavior, such as changes in customer traffic or the mix between on-premise and off-premise channels
for off-premise consumption, sales of which comprised approximately 22% of The Cheesecake Factory restaurant sales during fiscal 2023
our operating results were impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, causing supply chain challenges
contributing to significantly increased commodity and other costs
could lead to further shifts in consumer behavior, wage inflation, staffing challenges, product and services cost inflation
While we continue to operate in a competitive labor environment, we believe our people practices contribute significantly to our ability to attract talent
we implemented price increases above our historical levels, including an incremental price increase in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022, to help offset significant inflationary cost pressures
The average check for each customer, including beverages and desserts, was approximately $30.54 during fiscal 2023
Fluctuations in both average sales per location and average sales per productive square foot generally track with comparable restaurant sales trends

Average sales per productive square foot (defined as all interior square footage plus seasonally adjusted exterior patio square footage) for restaurants open for the full year were approximately $1,132 for fiscal 2023
Average sales per location for The Cheesecake Factory restaurants open for the full year were approximately $12.2 million for fiscal 2023

results in a significant level of dessert sales, representing approximately 17% of The Cheesecake Factory sales during fiscal 2023
we have encountered delays in opening new restaurants primarily due to delays in permitting and landlord readiness, as well as supply chain challenges
Restaurant-level preopening costs for a typical location in an established market average approximately $1.0 million to $1.5 million
Total construction costs to build our restaurant premises average approximately $1,100 per interior square foot

require a period of time after reaching normal traffic levels to achieve their targeted restaurant-level margins due to actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies
labor productivity inefficiencies commonly associated with new, highly complex restaurants
we receive initial development fees, site and design fees and ongoing royalties based on our licensees’ restaurant sales
we leverage opportunities in the consumer packaged goods channel by partnering with various third-party manufacturers
selling cheesecakes and other baked products to external foodservice operators, retailers and distributors
The Cheesecake Factory restaurants’ historically industry-leading retention rates
our ability to anticipate customer preferences and adapt our expansive menu to the latest trends

notes: Chunk contains a single event: part 1/6 of the FY2023 10-K (Item 1 Business + macro-impacts section); no fiscal_kpis present (only chunk_001 carries them).

Skipped as vague/non-reusable (5): 'geopolitical and macroeconomic events' (bare macro category), 'shifts in consumer behavior' (vague; covered by restaurant_traffic), new-unit 'honeymoon' sales pattern (instance pattern, not a reusable cause), 20% annual unit growth targets (banned word 'growth'; expansion covered by new_restaurant_development), 'competitive leasing terms'/'occupancy cost per seat' (marginal, no causal claim).
new_restaurant_development named with the source's own stable noun ('restaurant development time frames') to avoid banned state words 'opening'/'growth'.

Brand-level metrics (North Italia / Flower Child AUVs) appear only as descriptive statistics with no causal attribution beyond the company-wide AUV driver, so no separate brand drivers coined from this part.
